none.justification: Matches the GT on the required declaration (`include/linux/fs.h:1964`), definition (`fs/read_write.c:450`), public-header characterization, and `<linux/fs.h>` include. However it adds a false claim that `vfs_read` is `EXPORT_SYMBOL`'d/module-callable, directly contradicting the GT's verified export-status nuance.

ccodegraph.justification: Fully matches the GT: declaration at `include/linux/fs.h:1964`, definition at `fs/read_write.c:450`, correctly labels both, names `<linux/fs.h>`, and states it is public rather than `fs/internal.h`.

codegraph.justification: Fully matches the required GT locations and characterization: public declaration in `include/linux/fs.h:1964`, definition in `fs/read_write.c:450`, and typical include `<linux/fs.h>`. Extra call-graph details are not required and do not undermine the core answer.

cbm.justification: Correctly gives the declaration as `include/linux/fs.h:1964`, the definition as `fs/read_write.c:450`, labels them, and identifies `<linux/fs.h>` as the public header rather than an fs-internal header. The minor phrasing that the body starts at 451 is harmless because it clearly identifies the function definition line at 450.
